Source_Species.reason: The paper studies maize mutations and repeatedly refers to maize endosperm; source species is maize.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in maize lines and mutants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper concerns maize, explicitly Zea mays in the title.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Materials list W22 o2, W64A o2, and R802 o2, with corresponding wild types.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The mutations are directly studied for effects on zein accumulation/synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Zein is explicitly described as the major storage protein/prolamine fraction of maize endosperm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states zeins are deficient in lysine and tryptophan and that reduced zein content results in more nutritional grain.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results/introduction state zein accretions form in the lumen of rough ER cisternae and develop into vesicular protein bodies in endosperm.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state o2 shows major suppression of most 22.5-kDa zein polypeptides, with a few minor 19-kDa components also missing.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: o2 was examined by 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE (Fig. 1/2), in vitro translation/processing (Figs. 3/4), RNA blot hybridization (Fig. 5), and Southern analysis (Fig. 6).

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Endosperms were harvested 18–20 days after pollination, and functional assays were done on developing endosperm material.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper discusses o2 as a locus/mutation, proposed regulatory for zein synthesis, without identifying a specific protein product.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were self-pollinated and ears harvested 18–20 d after pollination; developing kernels/endosperm were analyzed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported: suppression of 22.5-kDa zeins in multiple backgrounds; mutant RER can process zeins; B14-homologous mRNAs are greatly reduced in o2; Southern analysis argues against a large deletion.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The statement that o2 had long been proposed as a regulatory locus is explicit, and improved nutritional quality due to zein reduction is stated. However, the claim of a 'reciprocal increase in non-zein storage proteins' is only discussed generally with citation to prior work, not directly demonstrated in this paper for o2.

Source_Species.reason: The mutation studied is in maize.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper title identifies the species as Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Materials specify W22 o7 and W22+.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: o7 is directly analyzed for effects on zein accumulation/synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Zein is explicitly a storage protein/prolamine fraction.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion generally states zeins are low in lysine and tryptophan and that reduced zein content improves grain nutritional quality; this applies to the class of mutations studied including o7.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper describes zein accumulation in endosperm protein bodies derived from rough ER vesicles.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state W22 o7 has some spots absent from both molecular weight size classes, with overall zein content known to be reduced.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: o7 is examined by 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE (Fig. 1/2), in vitro translation/processing (Figs. 3/4), and RNA blot hybridization (Fig. 5).

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Developing endosperms harvested 18–20 days after pollination were used.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper treats o7 as a mutation/locus and does not define a specific protein identity.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were self-pollinated and ears harvested 18–20 d after pollination.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper supports reduced total zein content, loss of some spots in both zein classes, competent mutant RER processing, and a more general effect than the class-selective o2 phenotype.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly contrasts o7 with o2, stating it lacks the class-specific suppression seen in o2 and appears to cause a general inhibition of synthesis of all zein classes.

Source_Species.reason: The mutation studied is in maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Materials specify W22 fl2 and W22+.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: fl2 is directly tested for effects on zein accumulation/synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Zein is explicitly described as a storage protein/prolamine.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion generally states zeins are low in lysine and tryptophan and reduction improves grain nutritional quality; this frames the mutant class including fl2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper describes zein deposition in protein bodies formed from rough ER in endosperm.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state W22 fl2 seems to be missing spots from only the lower molecular weight size class, and in vitro synthesis shows mostly 19-kDa zeins plus a minor band at ~25 kDa.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: fl2 is examined by 2D IEF:SDS-PAGE (Fig. 1/2), in vitro translation/processing (Fig. 3/4), and RNA blot hybridization (Fig. 5).

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Developing endosperm from kernels harvested 18–20 days after pollination was used.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper presents fl2 as a mutation/locus without identifying the underlying protein product.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were self-pollinated and ears were harvested 18–20 d after pollination.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The summary matches results/discussion: total zein is reduced, lower-MW components are missing on 2D gels, membranes process prezeins efficiently, and the phenotype is discussed as a general inhibition distinct from o2.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed treats fl2 as a core mutant affecting zein accumulation and does not identify the molecular gene product.
